violations.text: Lexans of pasta were observed stored in containers greater than 4 inches deep, stacked and covered. Food was observed at 70 degrees Fahrenheit. Foods were separated into smaller containers and placed with ice during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: 1. lexan food containers and lids 2. hot holding cabinet (parts on order per PIC) Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2025
Several floor tiles by the kettles were observed in poor repair. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2025
Food container lids in the walk-in cooler observed cracked/chipped. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/13/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the vent in the ware-washing room have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2023

Inadequate lighting observed in the walk-in cooler.Lighting 6-303.11 Intensity. The light intensity shall be: (A) At least 108 lux (10 foot candles) at a distance of 75 cm (30 inches) above the floor, in walk-in refrigeration units and dry FOOD storage areas and in other areas and rooms during periods of cleaning. -Lighting levels are specified so that sufficient light is available to enable employees to perform certain functions such as reading labels; discerning the color of substances; identifying toxic materials; recognizing the condition of food, utensils, and supplies; and safely conducting general food establishment operations and clean-up. Properly distributed light makes the need for cleaning apparent by making accumulations of soil conspicuous. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) observed with food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. -Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the can opener during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

Staff were observed not wearing a beard restraint. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair/beard from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the ware-washing area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the floors in the dry storage area have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2023
